explanation: The case satisfies all Section 2 criteria. Diagnosis (2.1): confirmed cervical radiculopathy at C5-C6 with nerve root compression and dermatomal symptoms. Imaging (2.3): MRI within 6 months, radiologist-interpreted, consistent with symptoms and surgical level. Conservative treatment: PT exceeds 6 weeks (7 weeks supervised, cervical-directed, with functional outcomes); pharmacotherapy includes two qualifying agents from different classes — naproxen (NSAID) for 6 weeks and gabapentin (neuropathic agent) for 5 weeks, both meeting the 4-week minimum (the policy requires at least two agents, not exclusion of others); interventional requirement met with one ESI. Physician documentation (2.4) includes all four required elements. All criteria are satisfied, so authorization is approved.
